cmd.purpose: Organisational root for all seven deployable binary entrypoints. Each child is a thin Wire+main.go launcher that delegates all business logic to openmeter/* domain packages and app/common; no domain logic or shared state lives here.

patterns.name: Wire-only entrypoint
patterns.description: Each cmd/* binary contains only wire.go (provider declarations with //go:build wireinject), wire_gen.go (generated), and main.go (config load + run.Group). Business logic belongs in openmeter/* or app/common.
patterns.example: cmd/server/wire.go declares wire.Build(common.Server, ...); cmd/server/main.go calls initializeApplication and delegates to common.Runner.

patterns.name: Post-migration provisioning before Run
patterns.description: Provisioning steps (EnsureBusinessAccounts, SandboxProvisioner, initNamespace) must execute after DB migration but before the run.Group starts consuming events.
patterns.example: cmd/billing-worker/main.go calls app.Migrate(ctx), then EnsureBusinessAccounts, then SandboxProvisioner, then run.Group.Run().

patterns.name: Namespace handler registration before initNamespace
patterns.description: In cmd/server, all namespace.Handler implementations (Ledger, KafkaIngest) must be registered via manager.RegisterHandler before initNamespace is called so the default namespace receives all handler provisioning.
patterns.example: cmd/server/main.go registers ledger handler, then kafkaIngest handler, then calls initNamespace(ctx, app.NamespaceManager).

patterns.name: oklog/run.Group for actor lifecycle
patterns.description: Long-running actors (HTTP server, Kafka consumer, telemetry server) are added to run.Group via common.Runner or manually with run.Add. No goroutine is spawned outside run.Group.

patterns.name: Metadata via common.NewMetadata
patterns.description: Each binary calls common.NewMetadata(version, binaryName) to populate OTel service.name and log fields. Version is read from ldflags or runtime/debug fallback.

key_file_guides.role: Declares the Wire provider graph for the binary. Contains //go:build wireinject and wire.Build call.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never remove //go:build wireinject. Adding a new domain service requires adding its provider set to wire.Build here.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by `go generate ./...` from wire.go. Do not edit manually.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any manual edit is overwritten on next `make generate`. Regenerate after every wire.go or constructor signature change.

key_file_guides.role: Registers namespace handlers, provisions default namespace and billing profile, builds router.Config, assembles run.Group with five actors.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Namespace handler registration order is load-bearing: Ledger must precede KafkaIngest; both must precede initNamespace.

key_file_guides.role: Provisions ledger business accounts and sandbox app before starting the Watermill consumer loop.
key_file_guides.watch_for: EnsureBusinessAccounts and SandboxProvisioner must complete before run.Group.Run(). Skipping EnsureBusinessAccounts leaves ledger without business accounts.

key_file_guides.role: Constructs Watermill Kafka subscriber in main.go (not Wire) so consumer group name can come from config at startup.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not move subscriber construction into Wire. Use app.EventPublisher.Marshaler() for event deserialization, not app.MessagePublisher.

key_file_guides.role: Wires entire application once via PersistentPreRunE; sub-commands use internal.App package-level globals.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not construct domain services inline in RunE. Always use cmd.Context() — never context.Background().

key_file_guides.role: Thin launcher: blank-imports plugin packages then calls service.RunCLI. No Wire DI.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New collector plugins must be added as blank imports here. Do not add Wire or domain service instantiation.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic directly in main.go instead of openmeter/* domain packages
Manually editing wire_gen.go — always regenerate via `make generate`
Registering a namespace.Handler after initNamespace in cmd/server — the default namespace will miss that handler's provisioning
Constructing domain services inline in cmd/jobs RunE instead of sourcing from internal.App
Introducing context.Background() anywhere other than the root context at program start in main()

decisions.decision: All business logic lives in openmeter/* or app/common; cmd/* is wiring and lifecycle only.
decisions.rationale: Keeps domain packages independently testable and reusable across binaries without importing cmd/*.

decisions.decision: Each binary has its own Wire provider graph in wire.go rather than sharing a single graph.
decisions.rationale: Different binaries need different subsets of providers; separate graphs prevent unintended coupling and reduce binary size.

decisions.decision: cmd/notification-service constructs its Watermill subscriber in main.go, not in Wire.
decisions.rationale: Consumer group name must come from config at startup, which is not available during Wire's compile-time graph resolution.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new binary entrypoint — wire.go provider declaration pattern

collector.purpose: Houses the Benthos/Redpanda Connect collector plugin surface (collector/benthos) and local demo environment (collector/quickstart). collector/benthos defines all custom Go plugins (input, bloblang, internal utils, leader-election service) that cmd/benthos-collector imports via blank imports; no openmeter domain logic is wired here.

patterns.name: Blank-import activation for plugin registration
patterns.description: Benthos plugins must be registered in init() before process start. cmd/benthos-collector imports sub-packages as _ to trigger init-time registration.

patterns.name: Leader-election gate on replicated work
patterns.description: All work that must not run on every replica checks services/leaderelection.IsLeader(res). Absent key defaults to true (safe solo deployment).
patterns.example: if !leaderelection.IsLeader(res) { return nil } // skip work on non-leader replicas

patterns.name: Environment-variable substitution in YAML presets
patterns.description: All YAML presets under presets/ and quickstart/ use ${VAR:default} substitution. No values are hardcoded.

patterns.name: Child-package specialisation
patterns.description: input/, bloblang/, internal/, services/, presets/ each own exactly one concern. Cross-cutting utils go to internal/ only.
patterns.example: collector/benthos/internal/shutdown/signaller.go — shutdown signal utility, not a plugin.

key_file_guides.role: Leader-election state stored in service.Resources generic map; IsLeader(res) helper reads it with absent-key=true default.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never read IsLeaderKey directly via res.GetGeneric — use IsLeader(res) to preserve absent-key=true semantics for single-replica deployments.

key_file_guides.role: Side-effect-only package: registers bloblang functions in init(). No exported symbols.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not export symbols from this package. Do not place business logic here.

key_file_guides.role: Internal message transaction utility. Pure utilities layer — no plugin registrations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add plugin registrations to internal/. Keep it as a pure utility layer.

key_file_guides.role: Demo stack that includes ../../quickstart/docker-compose.yaml rather than duplicating service definitions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not duplicate service definitions. Use in-memory dedupe cache (not Redis) in quickstart.

anti_patterns: Registering a plugin outside init() — Benthos requires init-time registration before process start
Adding business logic or plugin registrations to internal/ — pure utilities layer only
Reading IsLeaderKey directly via res.GetGeneric instead of using IsLeader(res) helper — misses absent-key=true default
Exporting symbols from collector/benthos/bloblang — side-effect-only package
Hardcoding OPENMETER_URL or OPENMETER_TOKEN in YAML configs instead of ${VAR:default} substitution

decisions.decision: Sub-package per concern (input, bloblang, internal, services, presets) rather than a flat package.
decisions.rationale: Benthos plugin types have distinct registration and lifecycle semantics; separating them prevents accidental cross-registration and clarifies what cmd/benthos-collector must blank-import.

decisions.decision: No Wire DI or openmeter domain service instantiation in collector/.
decisions.rationale: The Benthos framework provides its own component lifecycle; injecting openmeter Wire graphs would couple the collector to the full service dependency tree unnecessarily.

deploy.purpose: Kubernetes deployment artefacts for OpenMeter: two independently versioned Helm charts (openmeter, benthos-collector) published to GHCR OCI registry, plus a local kind cluster config for development. deploy/charts owns the shared helm-docs generation pipeline consumed by both charts.

patterns.name: helm-docs via shared template
patterns.description: Both chart READMEs are generated by helm-docs from deploy/charts/template.md and each chart's values.yaml comments. Never hand-edit a chart README.md.
patterns.example: make -C deploy/charts docs  # regenerates both chart READMEs from template.md + values.yaml annotations

patterns.name: OCI Helm registry distribution
patterns.description: Charts are packaged and pushed to ghcr.io OCI registry on release tags, not a traditional index-based Helm repository.

patterns.name: Enabled-guard + configmap override for optional infrastructure
patterns.description: Optional chart features (Kafka, ClickHouse, Redis bundled) use an enabled: bool guard in resource templates AND a corresponding override block in configmap.yaml.
patterns.example: {{- if .Values.kafka.enabled }}...{{- end }} paired with kafka section in configmap.yaml

key_file_guides.role: Drives helm-docs README generation for both charts.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a third chart requires a new README.tmpl.md and an update to this Makefile docs target.

key_file_guides.role: Shared helm-docs template consumed by both child charts.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Changes here affect both chart READMEs. Keep template generic — no chart-specific content.

key_file_guides.role: kind cluster config for local Kubernetes development with ingress port mappings (80/443).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Port mappings must match the Helm chart service/ingress defaults to avoid local routing mismatches.

key_file_guides.role: Renders the OpenMeter config.yaml from Helm values; all optional infrastructure blocks must have corresponding enabled guards.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing enabled guard in configmap.yaml for a new optional feature will inject config for disabled services.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing a child chart README.md — it is generated by helm-docs from template.md and values.yaml comments; edits will be overwritten on next `make docs`
Adding a third chart without a corresponding README.tmpl.md and Makefile update
Storing shared Kubernetes manifests (CRDs, cluster-scoped RBAC) at deploy/charts level — each chart must be fully self-contained
Publishing charts without aligning Chart.yaml appVersion with the container image tag in values.yaml

decisions.decision: Shared template.md at deploy/charts level rather than duplicated per chart.
decisions.rationale: Avoids README format drift between the two charts; single edit point for documentation structure.

decisions.decision: OCI registry (ghcr.io) as the distribution channel, not a traditional Helm repository index.
decisions.rationale: OCI registries provide content-addressable artefact storage and align with the container image distribution model already used for application images.

tools.purpose: Operational boundary between schema authorship and runtime startup: tools/migrate owns Atlas-generated SQL migrations, the golang-migrate runtime wrapper, and the viewgen sub-tool for ClickHouse view DDL. wait-for-compose.sh is a CI readiness gate. Nothing here contains business logic.

patterns.name: SourceWrapper for migration:ignore filtering
patterns.description: tools/migrate/fs.go wraps the embedded migration filesystem and filters out files tagged migration:ignore so Atlas lint files do not confuse golang-migrate.
patterns.example: migrate.New("iofs://migrations", db) uses the SourceWrapper to strip non-SQL files before applying.

patterns.name: OMMigrationsConfig as canonical migration config
patterns.description: All migration runtime configuration (DB URL, migration table name) flows through OMMigrationsConfig rather than ad-hoc viper reads.

patterns.name: Migration stop tests via runner/stops pattern
patterns.description: tools/migrate/migrate_test.go uses a runner/stops table pattern: each stop specifies an up-to version and a validation action on raw *sql.DB, with purgeDB between down and up passes to ensure idempotence.
patterns.example: stops := []migrationStop{{version: 20, action: func(db *sql.DB) error { /* assert schema shape */ }}}

patterns.name: viewgen for ClickHouse view DDL
patterns.description: tools/migrate/cmd/viewgen generates ClickHouse view SQL from Ent view schemas. Run it separately from `atlas migrate diff` because Atlas does not diff ent.View schemas.

patterns.name: wait-for-compose polling pattern
patterns.description: wait-for-compose.sh polls docker inspect health/state in a 60-attempt/2s loop before CI test steps proceed. Containers with no healthcheck fall through to state-based check.

key_file_guides.role: Runtime migration wrapper: embeds migrations FS, applies via golang-migrate, exposes Migrate(ctx, db) used at binary startup.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not call atlas migrate at runtime — only golang-migrate is used here. OMMigrationsConfig is the only config input.

key_file_guides.role: SourceWrapper that strips migration:ignore files from the embedded FS before golang-migrate sees them.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any new file added to migrations/ that is not a .up.sql/.down.sql must be tagged migration:ignore or it will confuse golang-migrate.

key_file_guides.role: Stop tests that verify schema shape at specific migration versions using raw *sql.DB.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use raw *sql.DB, not Ent — Ent only understands current schema. Call purgeDB between up and down passes.

key_file_guides.role: Asserts that views.sql matches what viewgen would generate from the current Ent schema.
key_file_guides.watch_for: If ent.View schema changes, run viewgen and commit the updated views.sql — this test will fail otherwise.

key_file_guides.role: Standalone tool that reads Ent schema paths and writes ClickHouse view DDL to views.sql.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not import app/common or openmeter/ domain packages — this tool only needs Ent schema paths.

key_file_guides.role: CI readiness gate: blocks until listed docker compose services are healthy or running.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Exit 1 on unhealthy state; timeout after 120s (60 attempts × 2s). Containers without healthcheck use state-based check.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing any file in tools/migrate/migrations/ — Atlas owns the chain and atlas.sum will fail CI validation
Calling `atlas migrate diff` expecting new ent.View schemas to appear — views require viewgen, not Atlas diff
Using Ent or any ORM inside migration stop test actions — raw *sql.DB is required because ORM only understands current schema
Adding a new migration stop test outside the runner/stops table pattern — it will not run purgeDB/down/up cycle
Importing app/common or openmeter/ domain packages from tools/migrate/cmd/viewgen/main.go

decisions.decision: golang-migrate wraps Atlas-generated SQL rather than using Atlas CLI at runtime.
decisions.rationale: Atlas CLI is a dev/CI tool; golang-migrate provides a stable embedded Go runtime for applying migrations at binary startup without Atlas binary dependency.

decisions.decision: viewgen is a separate tool from Atlas migration generation.
decisions.rationale: Atlas does not diff ent.View schemas into SQL migrations; ClickHouse view DDL requires custom generation and must be applied as an explicit SQL migration.

decisions.decision: wait-for-compose.sh is a standalone bash script rather than a Go helper.
decisions.rationale: It must run before any Go binary is available; bash docker-inspect polling has no runtime dependencies.
